notes: SHIP v1.14.266 - FLOOR TILE (per RULING-RACK-FLOOR-TILE.md, Option A). RESOLVES the open .262 floor question: the 3D rack floor becomes polished data-center TILE (0717 tile grid x 0718 reflection presence, in PHANTOM cyan/violet - never the refs blue/red). THREE edits in rackElevation_render3D: (1) BAY_REFLECT (window.devicePixelRatio<=2.1) -> true: the existing ghost clone IS the reflection and the ruling requires it visible in the floor on the iPhone (device-verify checks it) - was DPR-gated OFF at DPR>2.1 for perf; now on all devices, device-verify [6] checks 55fps for 2min. (2) floorMesh -> procedural anisotropic TILE: one CanvasTexture 512 (2x2 tiles) = charcoal ~#0a0d12 base + hairline near-black grout grid (2px) + deterministic per-tile luminance jitter (+/-3%) + faint noise; RepeatWrapping, repeat = (RH*5)/(RW*2) so one tile ~= rack width; anisotropy min(maxAniso,4) (THE detail that stops grout shimmer at grazing angles); registered via _tex() -> bayDispose. Material: map=tile, metalness 0.5, roughness 0.35, TRANSPARENT opacity 0.85, envMapIntensity 0.25; renderOrder 1 so it composites OVER the dim ghost clone -> reflection shows THROUGH the tile = SATIN (not mirror), strongest at the base, dissolving with distance; floor extent RH*5, existing black fog eats the far edge (no rim/horizon - zero-chrome boundary holds). (3) stamp. Option B (THREE.Reflector) NOT used (Option A shipped). Reflection inherits the .265 mirror finish (clone is post-build). Pad/ring/motes stay deleted (.262); void bg / mirror / gesture rulings unchanged - this only adds the floor. reh3d-only -> ?legacy=1 byte-identical; forge3d untouched. Gates: node --check 4/0; CSS balanced; NUL 0; ctrl unchanged; three-stamp .265->.266. NOT device-verified (cannot screenshot live 3D) - John iPhone gate: grout straight+hairline no shimmer; reflection stands in floor at base, satin; floor edge never visible (dissolves to void); per-tile variance reads material not checkerboard; tint cyan/violet only; 55+fps.
